yes: Keep your distance, mate. Identify yourself.
Military, eh? Stand down and state your business.
Another survivor from the forces? Interesting.
no: Stop right there. What do you want?
I wouldn't come any closer if I were you.

responses.text: Any advice for surviving out here?
Have you seen any other special forces?

dynamic_line: That's not important. Former military, let's leave it at that.
Used to be with 22 SAS. Not that it matters much now.
Does it matter? Just another survivor trying to stay alive.
Let's just say I had specialized training before the world went to hell.

responses.text: How did you end up here?

dynamic_line: Was. Past tense. Regiment doesn't exist anymore, far as I can tell.
Don't be. The skills that kept me alive then are keeping me alive now. Nothing special about it.
Save your admiration. Didn't do much good when the world fell apart, did it?
It's just training. Discipline and preparation. You'd do well to learn some of it yourself.

responses.text: Could you teach me some of those skills?

dynamic_line: Not interested in taking on students.
You learn by doing in this world. Stay alive long enough, you'll pick it up.
Maybe. If you prove you're worth the effort.
Training takes time we don't have. Best advice: stay alert, travel light, and trust no one.
Reconnaissance. Assessing threats. The usual.
Staying alive. Same as everyone else.
Keeping mobile. Stationary targets don't last long these days.
Looking for other survivors with military background. Strength in numbers, if they're the right sort.
That's not information I share casually.
Maybe. Maybe not. Better for both of us if you don't know.
Was part of a four-man patrol. I'm the only one still breathing.
Alone works better for me. Groups attract attention.
Was on an operation when everything went sideways. Details aren't important.
Joint training exercise with American Delta. Then the world ended.
Extraction went bad. Team was compromised. Then those... things started appearing.
Wrong place, wrong time. Story of my life.
Save it. Sympathy doesn't keep you alive out here.
It is what it is. Can't change the past.
Appreciate the sentiment, but it doesn't matter now.
We adapt and overcome. It's what we're trained to do.
Travel light. Stay quiet. Trust your instincts.
Avoid cities. Too many of those things. Stick to rural areas when possible.
Don't get attached to people or places. Be ready to move at a moment's notice.
Learn to live with less sleep. The night belongs to them now.
Avoid it when possible. Every engagement is a risk.
Noise attracts them. Use blades when you can, firearms only when necessary.
Always have an escape route planned. Never let them surround you.
Conserve ammunition. Make every shot count. Headshots are the only shots worth taking.
None. If they're smart, they're keeping a low profile.
Ran into an American operator a few weeks back. Heading west, last I saw.
There's rumors of a group operating out of an old RAF base, but I haven't confirmed it.
Special forces types tend to work alone or in small teams. They're out there, just hard to find.
